title: anthemis montana subsp. columnae
intro: Anthemis montana subsp. columnae, also known as the Alpine Chamomile or Mountain Mayweed, is a subspecies of the Anthemis montana plant, which belongs to the daisy family and is native to mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, insomnia, and anxiety. The health benefits of Anthemis montana subsp. columnae include reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and aiding in the treatment of menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to calm the mind and body, promote a good night's sleep, and soothe digestive problems.
